conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have a child - Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few more questions.

Do you live with Alex in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Based on what you've told me, let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

From the official guidance, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if:
- You're responsible for a child under 16 (or under 20 if in approved education or training)
- You live in the UK
- You live with the child or are contributing at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards their upkeep
- Only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child

Based on your situation:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16) ✓
- You live in the UK ✓
- Alex lives with you at home ✓
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex ✓

You meet all the basic eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news!** You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

You meet all the requirements because:
- Alex is 8 years old (under the age limit of 16)
- You live in the UK
- Alex lives with you
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK and Alex lives at home with them, establishing that they are responsible for the child. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, meeting the requirement that only one person can claim for each child. All eligibility criteria under UK Child Benefit rules are satisfied.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old child Alex. They meet all the standard eligibility requirements: they live in the UK, Alex is under 16 and lives with them, they are responsible for Alex, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for this child.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK and Alex lives at home with them, establishing that they are responsible for the child. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, meeting the requirement that only one person can claim for each child. All eligibility criteria under UK Child Benefit rules are satisfied.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old child Alex. They meet all the standard eligibility requirements: they live in the UK, Alex is under 16 and lives with them, they are responsible for Alex, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for this child.
